behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is handler->queue handoff plus HTTP status, not only HMAC function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP + real handler wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text is only '401' and 'not forwarded', with no real-network or model requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route in-process with real signature middleware and queue adapter; send mismatched body/signature; assert 401 and assert queue write method/store was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module ordering ('written to persistent queue before ... 200'). Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process server + real queue module (local persistence), no external OS/network dependency required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: text does not require real external HTTP/network effects, only ordering at module boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process request to handler with real queue implementation backed by test local store; instrument timestamps/order and assert queue persist completes before response is sent.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires interaction between request handler and async downstream worker lifecycle. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with controlled delayed downstream module in-process; no real subprocess/network needed. Rejected System: behavior text is about await/non-await semantics, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with downstream processor that blocks on a promise; send request; assert response returns before releasing processor promise; then confirm processing completes later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency; event-loop scheduling, socket handling, and timing jitter are real runtime effects. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/stubbed concurrency can pass while real runtime misses 3s under load. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text requires real 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under concurrent slow work, which is an OS/runtime boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start real Node process and send concurrent HTTP requests while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work; measure real elapsed time and assert each acknowledgment latency stays <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness depends on handler->idempotency store/queue interaction and state handoff across requests. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store is sufficient; no external network/subprocess required. Rejected Unit: pure function test would miss persistence/state interaction across two requests.
behaviors.test_description: Issue two webhook requests with same event ID against wired handler + real local persistence/queue; assert first is enqueued and second is rejected/skipped without second queue write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is mapping queue backpressure signal to HTTP contract (status + header) across module boundary. REAL-INFRA: can be tested with real queue module in full state or deterministic in-process adapter; no OS/network/subprocess necessity. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network failures or OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Drive queue to full state (or use real queue configured tiny capacity) and send webhook; assert HTTP 503 and Retry-After header is present with expected value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: mocking queue would hide real capacity enforcement bugs; must test real queue implementation with its local persistence. REAL-INFRA: still local DB/filesystem class of dependency, which fits Integration per level defs. Rejected System: behavior text says 'persistent queue ... enforces configured capacity' but does not require external network/subprocess/OS-specific event behavior beyond local storage.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with small configured capacity in test environment; push events until limit and assert overflow write is rejected and queue size remains at configured max.
